item.title: A 180% Crypto Rally Shows New Investing Era as Bitcoin Stumbles
item.description: As billions of dollars leave Bitcoin and Ether funds, money is flowing into a corner of crypto that promises something investors have long struggled to find in digital assets: a clearer path from economic activity to token value.

item.title: Meta Sells AI Agent for Businesses in Push to Monetize Service
item.description: Meta Platforms Inc. is selling businesses access to an artificial intelligence agent for the first time, its latest effort to generate revenue to offset the company’s hefty AI investments.

item.title: Kenya to Press Ahead With US Ebola Center, Health Secretary Says
item.description: Kenya will continue work on an isolation and treatment unit at an airbase in the East African nation that can be used to house US servicemen exposed to the Ebola virus, its health secretary said.

item.title: Vedanta Unit Targets IPO in New York to Fund Zambia Copper Mines
item.description: A Vedanta Resources Ltd. unit has applied to sell its shares in New York as part of efforts to raise money for the Zambian copper mining complex it owns.

item.title: Zambia Set for Talks With Bondholders Blocking Its Debt Buyback
item.description: Zambia’s government is heading to the negotiating table after a group of bondholders blocked its attempt to buy back a $1.36 billion bond before a potentially costly coupon increase.

item.title: Bank of America Bucks AI Job Fears With 2,000 Summer Interns
item.description: Bank of America Corp. is continuing to hire even as artificial intelligence and other technology tools take over jobs and cut off many traditional career paths.

item.title: AI Funding Boom Reaches Muni Market With Google-Tied Deal
item.description: Google parent Alphabet Inc. is poised to enter the municipal-bond market’s prepaid energy space by participating in a $1 billion transaction out of California, a major development in the evolution of a booming segment.

item.title: US Stock Futures Fall as US-Iran Flareup Threatens Peace Hopes
item.description: The rally in US stocks stalled before the bell on Wednesday as investors worried about the continued strain on a fragile ceasefire agreement between the US and Iran.

item.title: Options Traders Look Past Jobs Data as Inflation Takes Spotlight
item.description: Wall Street traders expect a strong jobs report on Friday. That may not be enough to soothe them, as the war-fueled oil price shock increasingly shifts the focus toward inflation.

item.title: Clayton Says Private Credit Can Do Better Job at Transparency
item.description: The New York City skyline Photographer: Jeenah Moon/Bloomberg

item.title: Oil’s Hormuz Alternatives Offer Only a Partial Fix
item.description: Pipelines and ports provide a critical bypass, but not necessarily a sufficient long-term solution should the Iran threat persist.

item.title: US Companies Add 122,000 Jobs, Most Since January 2025
item.description: US companies in May added the most jobs since January 2025, signaling the labor market may be gaining momentum despite rising energy costs sparked by the Iran war.

item.title: Europe Unveils Sweeping Tech Sovereignty Plan to Boost Chips, AI
item.description: The European Union unveiled a sweeping plan to expand its domestic technology supply chains, targeting greater independence from the US and Asia in semiconductors, artificial intelligence and cloud computing.

item.title: Foreigners Derail Historic Brazil Stock Rally They Once Fueled
item.description: Foreign investors pulled money out of Brazilian stocks at the fastest pace in more than six years in May, derailing what had been a historic run for the nation’s stock market.

item.title: Sitecore Said to Acquire Scrunch for $225 Million
item.description: Sitecore A/S, the digital-experience software company, has acquired Scrunch, a customer-experience platform that helps brands improve how they appear in AI-generated search results, according to a statement reviewed by Bloomberg.

item.title: 23andMe Is Back as Nonprofit Aiming to Reach 100 Million Users
item.description: The founder of 23andMe Research Institute wants to reach 100 million users, an ambitious goal after the seller of DNA testing kits emerged from bankruptcy as a nonprofit.

item.title: Kuwait to Look at Expanding Global Oil Storage After Iran War
item.description: Kuwait will consider increasing oil storage abroad to boost its ability to supply global markets after the war in the Persian Gulf blocked flows through the Strait of Hormuz, according to the head of the state energy company’s marketing arm.

item.title: US Premarket Movers for June 3, 2026
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures are little changed as of 7:50 a.m. in New York as growing strains on the ceasefire between the US and Iran sent oil prices higher.

item.title: Trump Officials Held Millions of Dollars of SpaceX Ahead of IPO
item.description: SpaceX’s initial public offering will likely make President Donald Trump’s already wealthy administration even richer.

item.title: AI Jitters Fuel Biggest Swings in India’s IT Stocks Since 2020
item.description: Shares of Indian software services companies are experiencing their sharpest swings since the pandemic, as the growing prowess of artificial intelligence fuels investor anxiety about the industry.

item.title: Russia Says FPV Drone Production Has Reached 15,000 Craft a Day
item.description: Russia has increased the production of first-person view drones by roughly 30-fold over the last three years, with manufacturers now capable of supplying more than 15,000 units a day, a top government official said.

item.title: Switzerland Still in Talks With US as Trump Proposes New Tariffs
item.description: Switzerland said it remains in talks with the US after Washington proposed fresh tariffs on imports from major trading partners, adding that negotiations to finalize a broader trade deal are continuing.

item.title: News Publishers Weigh Whether AI is Industry Killer or Savior
item.description: The potential effects of AI dominate a meeting of global publishers

item.title: Bessent Plays Down a $200 Gasoline-Bill Hike Hurting US Buying Power
item.description: The added US gas burden is one part of an increase in the cost of living that’s outpacing wages.

item.title: EU Warns Bulgaria of Excessive Deficit Months After Joining Euro
item.description: The European Union recommended adding Bulgaria to a list of countries violating budget balance rules just six months after the Balkan country adopted the euro.

item.title: EU Allows Room for Energy Crisis Aid, But With a Green Emphasis
item.description: The European Union will give extra budget leeway to member states to cope with high energy prices stoked by the Iran war, so long as it helps curb consumption of fossil fuels.

item.title: Global Businesses Worry About Fresh Dose of US Tariff Complexity
item.description: The proposed duties will go through a public comment period and bilateral talks before implementation

item.title: JPMorgan’s Lipikhina Sees Earnings Supercycle Driving US Stocks
item.description: An earnings “supercycle” in the US is poised to drive stocks to more record highs, according to Nataliia Lipikhina at JPMorgan Chase & Co., fueled by heavy spending from hyperscalers and advances in agentic AI.

item.title: Korea, Taiwan Stock Rallies Fail to Wow Foreign Investors
item.description: Cash is flowing into Japanese equities.

item.title: Hedge Fund Targets Ferrari Owners in Private Credit Exotics Boom
item.description: Private credit’s getting into the luxury car business.
